task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl main window shows 'usa.gdt' loaded in the title bar or data window, with variables like 'gdp' and 'inf' listed.

task_completion.description: Calculate the quarterly growth rate of real GDP using log-differences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new variable representing the log-difference of GDP (e.g., 'ldiff_gdp' or similar) appears in the Gretl variable list, or a dialog box shows the formula being applied to 'gdp'.

task_completion.description: Define a dummy variable 'recession' for periods where GDP growth is negative.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new variable named 'recession' appears in the Gretl variable list, or a 'Define new variable' dialog shows a formula setting the variable to 1 when the GDP growth variable is less than 0.

task_completion.description: Conduct an OLS regression of Inflation (inf) against the 'recession' dummy and a constant.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gretl model output window displays an OLS regression with 'inf' as the dependent variable and 'recession' (along with 'const') as the independent variables.

A 'Save as' dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/recession_results.txt', or a terminal/file manager confirms the file exists at this exact path containing the regression output.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Gretl GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Gretl menus, dialog boxes, and model windows being actively used, rather than just a terminal executing a pre-written script.

integrity.description: The regression results appear to be genuinely generated by Gretl.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OLS output window contains standard Gretl formatting (coefficients, std. errors, t-ratios, p-values, R-squared) and does not look like manually typed text.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste a mock result into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final saved text file contains the full, formatted Gretl model output, matching the results seen in the Gretl GUI model window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: usa.gdt. Output path: /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/recession_results.txt. Dependent variable: inf. Dummy variable name: recession.
